FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about research compounds, purity standards, and how Stratum operates.

Peptides are short chains of amino acids — the building blocks of proteins. They are naturally occurring molecules that serve as signaling agents in biological systems, influencing processes such as hormone secretion, cellular repair, immune modulation, and neurotransmission. In research contexts, synthetic peptides are used to study these mechanisms in controlled laboratory settings.
Stratum Peptides sells research compounds exclusively for in-vitro laboratory and research use. These compounds are not approved by the FDA for human or animal consumption. They are legal to purchase as research chemicals in the United States. It is the buyer's responsibility to understand and comply with their local laws and regulations. All purchases require agreement to our terms of use confirming research-only intent.
"Research purposes only" means these compounds are intended strictly for scientific study, laboratory experimentation, and academic investigation — not for human or animal consumption of any kind. Purchasers must be qualified researchers, licensed scientists, or otherwise authorized research personnel operating within an appropriate facility. These compounds are not drugs, supplements, or therapeutic products.
Yes — every order includes a Certificate of Analysis (COA) from an independent, ISO-accredited third-party laboratory. The COA details compound identity, purity percentage, test methodology (typically HPLC), and the testing date. Your product also includes a unique QR batch code that links directly to your COA online for instant verification.
Lyophilized (freeze-dried) peptides should be stored at −20°C (−4°F) in a sealed, light-protected container. Once reconstituted, store at 2–8°C (36–46°F) and use within 28 days. Avoid repeated freeze-thaw cycles. All products ship with a protocol card detailing specific storage conditions for each compound. Improper storage may degrade compound integrity and invalidate research data.
Scan the QR code on your product label using any smartphone camera — it will redirect you to your batch's live COA page. Alternatively, enter your batch code (printed on the label, e.g. STR-2026-XXX-0000) in the Batch Verification tool above. You'll instantly see your compound, purity result, test date, and lab name. No login or account required.

IMPORTANT LEGAL NOTICE: All products sold by Stratum Peptides are intended strictly for in-vitro research and laboratory use only. These compounds are not approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) or any regulatory authority for human or animal consumption, therapeutic use, dietary supplementation, or any clinical application.

These statements have not been evaluated by the Food and Drug Administration. These products are not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease. Stratum Peptides makes no representations or warranties regarding the safety or efficacy of these compounds for any use other than scientific research.

By purchasing from Stratum Peptides, you confirm that you are 18 years of age or older, a qualified researcher or licensed scientist, and that you understand and agree to use these compounds solely for lawful research purposes. Resale for human or animal consumption is strictly prohibited. You accept full responsibility for compliance with all applicable local, state, and federal laws and regulations.

Stratum Peptides shall not be liable for any misuse of these compounds. All sales are final for research compounds.
